sections.title: Market Pulse: Geopolitical Tensions Weigh on Indices; Mixed Futures Point to Caution

items.headline: U.S. Futures Mixed Amid Middle East Tensions After S&P 500 Breaks Nine-Day Streak
items.summary: U.S. stock futures presented a mixed picture early Thursday, with Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 futures declining, while Dow Jones Industrial Average futures showed slight gains. This follows a broad market selloff on Wednesday, triggered by rising Middle East tensions, which notably ended the S&P 500 Index's nine-session winning streak. Market sentiment, as indicated by the 'Fear and Greed index', shifted from 'Greed' to 'Neutral' on Wednesday. Oil prices, after a surge on Wednesday due to U.S.-Iran hostilities, edged lower on Thursday following a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon, though the broader regional conflict remains unresolved. Asian markets retreated, while European stocks advanced.

items.statContext: Down 0.7% on Wednesday, snapping a nine-day winning streak.

items.headline: Laser Photonics Corporation Leads Monthly Gains
items.summary: Laser Photonics Corp. ($LASE) has seen a significant monthly gain, currently up +237.47% as of early June 4, 2026.

items.headline: Julong Holding Limited Continues Strong Performance
items.summary: Julong Holding Limited ($JLHL) recorded an impressive monthly increase of +82.22% as of early June 4, 2026.

items.headline: VIDA Global Inc. Shows Robust Growth
items.summary: VIDA Global Inc. ($VIDA) surged by +54.85% in early June, marking it as a top monthly performer.

items.headline: Broadcom Tanks on Disappointing Earnings
items.summary: Shares of Broadcom Inc. ($AVGO) plummeted approximately 12% in pre-market trading on Thursday following its fiscal second-quarter earnings report, which reportedly included disappointing revenues. Some reports indicated a drop of nearly 14% in extended trading on Wednesday.

items.headline: CrowdStrike Falls After Outlook Release
items.summary: CrowdStrike Holdings Inc. ($CRWD) saw its stock drop over 10% in pre-market trading on Thursday after releasing its latest outlook. This follows a decline of more than 21% on Wednesday.

items.headline: HiTek Global Inc. Leads Monthly Declines
items.summary: HiTek Global Inc. ($HKIT) recorded the biggest monthly decline so far in June 2026, falling -90.58%.

items.headline: Consumer Durables, Healthcare Lead Sector Gains; IT and Metals Lag
items.summary: On Thursday, consumer-focused and healthcare segments emerged as leading sectors, with Nifty Consumer Durables gaining 1.31% and Nifty MidSmall Healthcare advancing 0.81%. Conversely, Information Technology and Metal stocks experienced pressure, with Nifty Metal declining 0.58% and Nifty IT falling 0.42%. This divergence reflects selective buying in defensive sectors amidst geopolitical concerns, while cyclical sectors like technology and metals face headwinds.

items.headline: Jobless Claims and Productivity Data Expected Today; ISM Services PMI Beats Estimates
items.summary: Investors are awaiting the release of initial jobless claims and Q1 U.S. productivity data today. In other economic news, the ISM Services PMI for May rose to 54.5, exceeding market expectations and marking the strongest expansion in the services sector in three months. The labor market appears stable with the unemployment rate around 4.3% for the past year.

items.headline: Fed Officials to Speak; Inflation Remains Above Target
items.summary: Richmond Fed President Tom Barkin and Kansas City Fed President Jeff Schmid are scheduled to speak today. Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan highlighted on Wednesday that inflation, measured by PCE, is still running close to 4% over the past 12 months, taking too long to return to the FOMC's 2% target. The FOMC has held the federal funds rate unchanged at 3.50% to 3.75% so far this year.

items.headline: Broadcom and CrowdStrike Lead Post-Earnings Drops; Other Major Companies Report
items.summary: Broadcom ($AVGO) and CrowdStrike ($CRWD) experienced significant share price declines after their respective earnings reports and outlooks. Other major companies scheduled to report earnings today include Ciena ($CIEN), DocuSign ($DOCU), Lululemon Athletica ($LULU), and Planet Labs ($PL). PVH Corp. ($PVH) is also holding a conference call today to discuss its first-quarter results.

items.headline: Geopolitical Risks and AI Exuberance Shape Market Landscape
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions, particularly renewed U.S.-Iran hostilities, continue to be a significant macro factor, driving risk-off sentiment and impacting oil prices. Meanwhile, the S&P 500's valuation is nearing dot-com bubble levels, fueled by exuberance over artificial intelligence and projected earnings growth. However, investors are keenly watching for revenue acceleration from AI investments. In international news, Russia's economic forum opens under the shadow of Ukrainian drone attacks.
